_readme: Candidate patterns noticed across calls. kind=coachable (lexical/structural, transferable via coaching) or dispositional (acoustic/trait, selection signal not coaching target). Every entry stays a hypothesis until validated: coachable -> pass-rate moves after a coaching action; dispositional -> within-rep variance is low across contexts AND coaching never moves it.

hypotheses.statement: High performers are consistently animated (high pitch variation, never monotone) - passion, not coachable.
hypotheses.notes: Shipped as 'engagement disposition' (disposition.py, 2026-07-10): rep-level axis from arousal history - mean, ceiling (does the rep EVER light up), fade rate. flat = ceiling<0.52 and mean<0.47 across 5+ calls -> flagged as dispositional, not a coaching target. Both current reps grade steady (Ed mean 0.504 ceiling 0.543; Max mean 0.491 ceiling 0.551). Supporting evidence for trait-hypothesis: cross-call arousal SD is tight (0.03-0.04) for both reps. Not yet observed: a flat rep (no negative example to calibrate against).
hypotheses.validation_plan: Thresholds are v1 heuristics. Validate when (a) a genuinely flat rep enters the sample, (b) outcome data ties disposition to win rates, (c) coaching attempts on any flat rep fail to move arousal (confirming non-coachability).

hypotheses.statement: Anchoring the value prop to the incumbent's budget with a quantified accuracy claim ('pay me what you pay ZoomInfo, 95-97% accurate on supply chain contacts') appears in the call with the strongest verbal buy-in (MODE).
hypotheses.validation_plan: Track presence of quantified incumbent-anchored value prop vs call outcome as call volume grows.

hypotheses.statement: Asking for the incumbent contract end date ('remind me when your zoom contract ends') creates a concrete timeline for the deal; present in MODE, absent in Flock which ended with a vague follow-up.
hypotheses.validation_plan: Track contract-date discovery vs whether a dated next step was secured.

hypotheses.statement: Seller lineup changes call quality: with Ed on the call, Max's discovery/progression passes 2.7x more often (48% joint vs 18% solo) but his value-prop rate drops (57% vs 71%, deferring the pitch to Ed). Strong-interest rate: Max solo 38% vs joint 30%.
hypotheses.source: SQL head-to-head over voice.db (2026-07-10), n=66 Max gradings (45 solo / 21 joint)
hypotheses.notes: Confounded by call stage - joint calls skew late-stage (contract/trial reviews) where next steps come naturally. Ed has only 1 solo call, so the comparison is Max-solo vs Max-with-Ed, not Ed vs Max. Validate by tagging call stage and re-cutting; if it holds, the coaching action is 'run discovery like a joint call' (specific behaviors from joint-call evidence quotes).
